stem: For the parabola \( y=[[a]]x^{2} \), the focus is at \( (0,p) \). Find \( p \), the focal length.
The parabola \( y=[[a]](x-[[h]])^{2}+[[k]] \) has vertex \( (h,k) \). Find \( k \).
solution: The vertex form \( y=a(x-h)^{2}+k \) directly shows the vertex is \( (h,k) \), so \( k=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This reports the \( x \)-coordinate of the vertex instead of the \( y \)-coordinate.
This has the wrong sign.
This adds the two vertex coordinates together.
This reports the leading coefficient instead of the vertex coordinate.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct value.

stem: The parabola \( y=[[a]](x-[[h]])^{2}+[[k]] \) has vertex \( (h,k) \). Find the \( y \)-coordinate of its focus.
solution: The focal length is \( p=\frac{1}{4a} \), and the focus sits \( p \) above the vertex, so its \( y \)-coordinate is \( k+\frac{1}{4a}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This moves the focus below the vertex instead of above.
This uses \( \frac{1}{2a}\) instead of \( \frac{1}{4a}\) for the focal length.
This forgets to add the vertex's \( y \)-coordinate.
This confuses the focal length formula with the latus rectum length \( 4a\).
This is off by one from the correct coordinate.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct coordinate.

stem: The parabola \( y=[[a]](x-[[h]])^{2}+[[k]] \) has vertex \( (h,k) \). Find the \( y \)-coordinate of its directrix.
solution: The directrix sits \( p=\frac{1}{4a} \) below the vertex, so its \( y \)-coordinate is \( k-\frac{1}{4a}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This places the directrix above the vertex instead of below (that would be the focus's side).
This uses \( \frac{1}{2a}\) instead of \( \frac{1}{4a}\) for the focal length.
This forgets to include the vertex's \( y \)-coordinate.
This confuses the focal length formula with the latus rectum length \( 4a\).
This is off by one from the correct coordinate.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct coordinate.

stem: For the parabola \( y=[[a]]x^{2}+[[b]]x+[[c]] \), find the \( x \)-coordinate of the vertex.
solution: The vertex of \( y=ax^{2}+bx+c \) has \( x=-\dfrac{b}{2a}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the required minus sign.
This forgets to double \( a\) in the denominator.
This inverts the roles of \( a\) and \( b\).
This drops both the minus sign and the factor of \( 2\).
This is off by one from the correct \( x\)-coordinate.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct \( x\)-coordinate.

stem: For the parabola \( y=[[a]]x^{2}+[[b]]x+[[c]] \), find the \( y \)-coordinate of the vertex.
solution: Substituting \( x=-\dfrac{b}{2a} \) into \( y=ax^{2}+bx+c \) gives \( y=c-\dfrac{b^{2}}{4a}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This adds the correction term instead of subtracting it.
This forgets to double \( a\) again after squaring \( b\), using \( 2a\) instead of \( 4a\).
This forgets to square \( b\) in the correction term.
This drops the required minus sign.
This is off by one from the correct \( y\)-coordinate.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct \( y\)-coordinate.

stem: For the sideways parabola \( x=[[a]]y^{2} \), the focus is at \( (p,0) \). Find \( p \), the focal length.
distractor_rationales: This forgets the factor of \( 4\) in the denominator.
This uses \( 2\) instead of \( 4\) in the denominator.
This inverts the correct fraction.
This uses \( a/4\) instead of \( 1/(4a)\).
This is off by one from the correct focal length.
This is off by a small amount (other direction) from the correct focal length.

stem: The sideways parabola \( x=[[a]](y-[[k]])^{2}+[[h]] \) has vertex \( (h,k) \) and opens to the right. Find the \( x \)-coordinate of its directrix.
solution: Since the parabola opens rightward, the directrix sits \( p=\frac{1}{4a} \) to the left of the vertex, so its \( x \)-coordinate is \( h-\frac{1}{4a}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This places the directrix on the focus's side instead of the opposite side.
This uses \( \frac{1}{2a}\) instead of \( \frac{1}{4a}\) for the focal length.
This forgets to include the vertex's \( x\)-coordinate.
This confuses the focal length formula with the latus rectum length \( 4a\).
This is off by one from the correct coordinate.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct coordinate.

stem: For the parabola \( y=[[a]]x^{2} \), find the length of the latus rectum (the focal chord perpendicular to the axis of symmetry).
solution: The focal length is \( p=\frac{1}{4|a|} \), and the latus rectum has length \( 4p=\frac{1}{|a|}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This reports the focal length \( p\) instead of the latus rectum length \( 4p\).
This inverts the correct value.
This is off by a factor of \( 2\) from the correct length.
This reports \( |a|\) itself rather than a function of it.
This is off by one from the correct length.
This is off by a small amount (other direction) from the correct length.

stem: The parabola \( x^{2}=4([[p]])y \) has focus \( (0,[[p]]) \). The point \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \) lies on the parabola. Find its distance to the focus.
solution: By the defining property of a parabola, the distance from any point on it to the focus equals its distance to the directrix \( y=-[[p]] \), which is \( [[y0]]-(-[[p]])=[[y0]]+[[p]]=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This subtracts the focal length instead of adding it.
This uses the point's \( x\)-coordinate instead of its \( y\)-coordinate.
This computes the distance from the point to the origin instead of to the focus.
This omits the focal-length shift entirely.
This is off by one from the correct distance.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct distance.
